questions.question: After poor sleep, how much more reactive does the amygdala become to negative stimuli?

options.text: Up to 20% more reactive
Up to 40% more reactive
Up to 60% more reactive
Up to 80% more reactive

questions.explanation: After poor sleep, the amygdala (your emotional alarm system) becomes up to 60% more reactive to negative stimuli, making you more irritable, anxious, and emotionally volatile.

questions.question: What happens to the prefrontal cortex after sleep deprivation?

options.text: It becomes hyperactive
Its connection to the amygdala weakens, reducing emotional regulation
It produces more melatonin
It shuts down completely

questions.explanation: Sleep deprivation weakens the connection between the prefrontal cortex and amygdala. This means your rational, calming voice becomes quieter, making it harder to regulate emotions, make decisions, or think clearly.

questions.question: What causes 'brain fog' after poor sleep?

options.text: Increased blood sugar levels
Reduced prefrontal cortex activity, incomplete memory consolidation, and less flexible thinking
Too much melatonin remaining in the system
Overactivity of the parasympathetic nervous system

questions.explanation: Brain fog results from reduced prefrontal cortex activity (difficulty concentrating), incomplete memory consolidation during deep sleep (forgetting things), and reduced cognitive flexibility (less creative problem-solving).

questions.question: What is the 'upward spiral' concept in sleep science?

options.text: Sleeping longer each night leads to needing less sleep
Better sleep leads to calmer mood, clearer focus, more energy, and even better sleep
REM sleep stages get progressively longer each night
Your circadian rhythm speeds up over time

questions.explanation: The upward spiral means improvements in sleep compound: better sleep leads to calmer mood, which leads to clearer focus, which leads to more energy, which leads to even better sleep.

questions.question: Why does sleep help with emotional regulation?

options.text: Sleep eliminates all negative emotions
Sleep restores the prefrontal cortex's ability to regulate the amygdala, keeping emotional responses proportionate
Sleep reduces the size of the amygdala permanently
Sleep replaces negative memories with positive ones

questions.explanation: After restorative sleep, the connection between the prefrontal cortex and amygdala is fully restored. This means your rational mind can effectively regulate emotions, support clear decision-making, and maintain composure under stress.

title: Sleep for Mood, Focus, and Productivity - Quiz
